title: CIP-309 shared definitions
description: Shared $defs reused across the CIP-309 JSON Schema set: fixed-length byte-string types and the open algorithm-identifier string types for each registry. CBOR has a native byte-string type; JSON does not. Every CBOR `bstr` is therefore modelled here as a lowercase-hex JSON string whose character count is twice the documented byte length (e.g. a 32-byte digest is a 64-character hex string). The algorithm-identifier `$defs` are deliberately OPEN strings: structural validation cannot decide registry membership (whether `sha2-256`, `xchacha20-poly1305`, etc. is registered) — that is a typed error-code check performed by the validator's domain pass against the extensible registries, proven by the conformance vectors. These schemas describe only the PERMISSIVE structural superset; cross-field invariants and per-algorithm digest/field lengths are not expressible here.

bytes32.title: 32-byte byte string (hex-encoded)
bytes32.description: A CBOR `bstr` of exactly 32 bytes, modelled as a 64-character lowercase-hex JSON string. Used for content-hash digests, Merkle roots, the supersedes tx hash, and slots_mac.

bytes48.title: 48-byte byte string (hex-encoded)
bytes48.description: A CBOR `bstr` of exactly 48 bytes (32-byte content-encryption key + 16-byte AEAD tag), modelled as a 96-character lowercase-hex JSON string. Used for the per-slot `wrap` field on every KEM.

content-hash-alg.title: Content-hash algorithm identifier
content-hash-alg.description: Named identifier of a content-hash algorithm from the extensible hash-algorithm registry (e.g. sha2-256, blake2b-256). OPEN string here: registry membership (UNSUPPORTED_HASH_ALG) and the per-algorithm digest length (HASH_DIGEST_LENGTH_MISMATCH) are not structural and are checked by the validator's domain pass.

merkle-commit-alg.title: Merkle list-commitment algorithm identifier
merkle-commit-alg.description: Named identifier of a Merkle list-commitment algorithm from the extensible registry (e.g. rfc9162-sha256). OPEN string here: registry membership (UNSUPPORTED_MERKLE_COMMIT_ALG) is a domain-pass check.

aead-alg.title: Content AEAD algorithm identifier
aead-alg.description: Named identifier of an authenticated-encryption cipher from the extensible AEAD registry (e.g. xchacha20-poly1305). OPEN string here: registry membership (UNSUPPORTED_AEAD_ALG), the unauthenticated-cipher denylist (UNAUTHENTICATED_CIPHER_FORBIDDEN), and the per-AEAD nonce length (NONCE_LENGTH_MISMATCH) are domain-pass checks.

kem-alg.title: KEM algorithm identifier
kem-alg.description: Named identifier of a key-encapsulation mechanism from the extensible KEM registry (e.g. x25519, mlkem768x25519). OPEN string here: registry membership and the KEM-driven slot shape (ENC_SLOT_INVALID_SHAPE, KEM_*_LENGTH_MISMATCH) are domain-pass checks — a slot cannot see the envelope-level kem in isolation.

kdf-alg.title: Passphrase KDF algorithm identifier
kdf-alg.description: Named identifier of a password-based key-derivation function from the extensible KDF registry (e.g. argon2id). OPEN string here: registry membership (ENC_PASSPHRASE_ALG_UNSUPPORTED) and the per-KDF params shape/floor (ENC_PASSPHRASE_ARGON2_PARAMS_TOO_LOW) are domain-pass checks.
